report.summary: Researched the $4B+ Bitcoin liquid staking token market, wrote a comprehensive 3100-word intermediate guide comparing LBTC, SolvBTC, and pumpBTC, optimized SEO with Article + HowTo + FAQ structured data, scaffolded the Next.js page, built an interactive BTC LST yield estimator component, and added the page to PUBLISH_QUEUE.

sections.title: Research Brief
items: Bitcoin LST market hit ~$4B TVL in early 2026; Lombard LBTC holds ~60% market share at ~$2B peak TVL
Solv Protocol SolvBTC is the closest competitor at ~$1.96B TVL across 6+ chains, with 30,000+ active users
pumpBTC is the simpler entrant at ~$500M TVL; focuses on clean UX without multi-strategy vault complexity
Babylon Protocol underpins all major LSTs; direct native staking yields 0.5–1% APY in BABY tokens
DeFi yield stacking (Aave, Morpho, Pendle) can push blended LST APY to 2–5% but adds composability risk
Key risks: bridge/cross-chain exploits, smart contract bugs, LST depeg events, BABY token price volatility, upcoming BARD token unlocks in March 2026
Gap identified: degen0x had babylon-protocol-bitcoin-staking and btcfi-bitcoin-defi-guide-2026 but no standalone BTC LST comparison guide
Topic selected: bitcoin-liquid-staking-tokens-guide-2026 — strongest market size and clearest content gap

sections.title: Content Created
items: 3100-word intermediate-level learn guide covering 8 numbered sections with Table of Contents
Section 1: What Is a Bitcoin Liquid Staking Token? (concept explanation + key concept callout box)
Section 2: How Bitcoin LSTs Work Under the Hood (5-step deposit-to-redemption flow cards)
Section 3: Top Bitcoin LST Protocols Compared (comparison table + 3 protocol deep-dive cards for Lombard, Solv, pumpBTC)
Section 4: Yield Sources (4 yield layers with APY ranges and sustainability ratings)
Section 5: Bitcoin LSTs vs WBTC vs Native Staking (5-row comparison table)
Section 6: Risks You Need to Understand (6 risk cards: bridge, smart contract, depeg, slashing, reward token, custodian)
Section 7: How to Get Started (5-step guide + internal links to related guides and tools)
Section 8: FAQ — 6 questions targeting People Also Ask search intent
Market stats hero bar: ~$4B TVL, ~60% Lombard share, ~$1.96B Solv, 57K+ BTC staked

sections.title: SEO Optimization
items: Title tag: 'Bitcoin Liquid Staking Tokens (LSTs) Guide 2026 | degen0x' — 57 chars (under 60 limit)

Meta description: 'Compare top Bitcoin LSTs: LBTC (Lombard), SolvBTC, and pumpBTC. Yields, risks, DeFi integrations, and how to choose the right protocol for your BTC strategy.' — 157 chars (140-160 target)
OpenGraph type: article with publishedTime/modifiedTime 2026-03-17
Twitter card: summary_large_image
Structured data: Article schema (wordCount 3100, datePublished/Modified 2026-03-17)
Structured data: HowTo schema — 5-step 'How to Get Started with Bitcoin LSTs' (PT2H total time)
Structured data: FAQ schema — 6 questions targeting high-value search queries
All three schemas combined via combineSchemas() and rendered via StructuredData component
Breadcrumb component included (auto-generates BreadcrumbList JSON-LD)
5 internal links: babylon-protocol-bitcoin-staking, btcfi-bitcoin-defi-guide-2026, restaking-eigenlayer-guide, tools/staking-apy, tools/yield-farming-calculator
Keywords array: bitcoin liquid staking, bitcoin LST, LBTC Lombard, SolvBTC, pumpBTC, bitcoin yield 2026, BTCfi
Page added to PUBLISH_QUEUE in src/lib/published-pages.ts under Build Cycle 2026-03-17

sections.title: Interactive Component
items: Component: BitcoinLSTYieldEstimator.tsx (client component, ~350 lines TypeScript)
Features: BTC amount input, live USD value display, BTC price input, quick-select buttons (0.01/0.1/0.5/1/5 BTC)
Protocol selector: Lombard (LBTC), Solv Protocol (SolvBTC), pumpBTC — each with token badge, color coding, chain info
Yield layer toggles: checkbox UI for stacking strategies; Babylon base layer required, DeFi layers optional
Results panel: Total APY range, Annual Yield in BTC, Annual Yield in USD, Monthly Yield in BTC
Sustainability ratings per layer: High/Medium/Low with color coding
Component embedded in guide page between Section 3 (Protocol Comparison) and Section 4 (Yield Sources)
TypeScript: zero new errors introduced in created files
